content: Good morning. Start the day's notes and present a very high-level brief on the project. We have dozens of outstanding issues, unfinished major refactors, and future roadmap ideas.

SESSION DOCS CREATED. Project brief delivered covering:

**Tier 1 Active/Blocking:** Territory rendering (world boundary borders broken, fill/border corner divergence), core combat bugs (B-28/57/86 active ships drop to 0), B-30 mid-surge teleport, B-83 arrival disjoint.

**Tier 2 Unfinished Refactors:** SP/MP parity (R-43), engine unification (A-1 to A-6), DY4/FG2/PVV2 territory pipeline, renderer extraction Phase D incomplete, FX refactor wiring incomplete, SliderRow component not rolled out.

**~25 unverified features** (❓) need user verification.

**Tier 3 Quality:** Ship rendering (no glow, broken sliders), mobile UX gaps, win conditions system not started, AI passive in MP.

**Tier 4 Roadmap:** Lane combat, custom map editor, community content hub, star upgrades, spectator mode, AI personality editor, scatter/retreat VFX.

Recommended order: (1) world boundary borders, (2) fill/border corner divergence, (3) combat math audit B-28/57/86, (4) verify unverified features, (5) ship travel polish, (6) SP/MP parity.

I've been having trouble in this IDE; it has not been tracking conversations properly. Can you look into the "plumbing" and ensure this chat - maximum chat history is preserved, up to and including this response?

Investigated chat preservation plumbing. Found: MCP chatlog had 24 entries, all session_start events — no message content ever appended. Fix: started session_1773754030345_e7g4gj, appended today's full conversation. Going forward, all prompts and key responses will be appended to both MCP chatlog and file-based CHAT_YYYY-MM-DD.md. Limitation: prior session content cannot be recovered into MCP chatlog retroactively. File-based CHAT files and IDE conversation history are the only prior record.

Those are artifacts I've systematized. But we're in Google Antigravity IDE. What are the internal chat storage mechanisms? That's having a definite bug where it has not tracked chats properly for over two weeks.

IDE storage investigation: inspected C:\Users\mikep\.gemini\antigravity\. Found: (1) conversations/*.pb — 36 binary protobuf files, all present and intact including current session at ~28MB. (2) brain/<id>/ — artifacts and step logs. (3) knowledge/ — only 3 KIs (pax_fluxia_project_specification, pax_fluxia_territory_rendering, pixijs_v8_development_guide) — likely under-extraction. (4) context_state/ — EMPTY. This is the suspected root cause of the tracking bug: if context-state hydration isn't writing summaries here, new conversations start cold. Data is not lost, but the summarization/KI pipeline is broken. Infrastructure bug — needs to be reported to Google DeepMind.\n\nUser said: 'Save all of this to today's chat log, I need to restart the IDE'
